title: Listening - Part 4: Learning a New Language
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about learning a foreign language as an adult. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I took up Mandarin because [21]the firm I work for was opening a branch in Shanghai, and I didn't want to be the only one relying on a translator[/21]. I wanted to show some respect. It’s been brutal, honestly. But [26]what I’ve really gained is a far deeper appreciation for my own native tongue[/26]. You don't really look at the mechanics of your own language until you try to learn one that works in a completely different way. || Speaker 2: My motive for learning Italian was purely romantic. [22]I’d fallen for someone from Rome and I wanted to be able to talk to their parents without looking like an idiot[/22]. It didn't work out with the partner, unfortunately, but I kept up with the classes. [27]I’ve found that it’s opened up a whole new world of literature that I never would have accessed otherwise[/27]. Reading Dante in the original is a spiritual experience. || Speaker 3: I’m a neuroscientist, and [23]I started learning Arabic as a bit of a self-experiment to see if it really does delay cognitive decline[/23]. I wanted to keep my brain sharp. It’s definitely challenging. But the biggest surprise has been [28]the boost in my social confidence[/28]. Making mistakes in front of a class and laughing about it has made me much less of a perfectionist in my professional life. || Speaker 4: I’ve always been a bit of a traveller, and [24]I felt ashamed that I could only speak English despite having visited twenty countries[/24]. It felt arrogant. So, I signed up for Portuguese. [29]The most rewarding thing has been the way it’s changed my travel experiences[/29]. Instead of staying in hotels, I now stay in local guest houses and have real conversations with people. It’s much more immersive. || Speaker 5: I’m a singer, and [25]I needed to learn German specifically so I could perform Lieder with the correct emotional nuance[/25]. You can't just phonetically copy the sounds. What I didn't expect was [30]the mental discipline it required[/30]. Having to memorise declensions and complex grammar has made me much more focused and patient in other areas of my life. It’s been a lesson in perseverance.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) why each speaker decided to learn a new language.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 wanted to avoid relying on a translator for their company's new branch.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 wanted to communicate with the parents of someone they had 'fallen for'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to see if learning a language would 'delay cognitive decline'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 felt 'ashamed' and 'arrogant' for only speaking English while travelling.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 needed German to perform songs with the 'correct emotional nuance'.

options.A: to overcome a feeling of embarrassment
options.B: to build a relationship with a partner's family
options.D: to prepare for a permanent move abroad
options.G: to investigate a personal theory about health
options.H: to obtain a specific academic qualification

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has found most rewarding about the process.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 gained a 'deeper appreciation' for the mechanics of their own language.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 values being able to read Italian literature in its 'original' form.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 feels more 'social confidence' and less like a perfectionist.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 enjoys having 'real conversations' and 'immersive' travel experiences.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 values the 'mental discipline', focus, and patience they developed.

options.A: a reduction in their fear of failure
options.B: the chance to mentor other students
options.C: an improvement in their ability to concentrate
options.D: a better understanding of their own language
options.E: the financial benefits it has brought
options.F: the opportunity for more authentic interactions
options.G: a sense of pride in their achievement
